text.title: growth-webinar-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-webinar-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Generic topic with no specific problem statement (e.g., "How to grow your business").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Broad topics attract nobody with a pressing pain; specific problems ("5 mistakes killing your X") attract the exact ICP.

failure-mode.description: Starting promotion fewer than 10 days before the event.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-channel cadence requires 3 weeks; compressed timelines produce fewer than 30% of potential registrations.

failure-mode.description: No partner outreach — relying only on owned channels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Partner cross-promotions deliver 10-30% of registrations at zero cost; skipping them caps reach.

failure-mode.description: Complex registration form (company, role, phone, more than 2 fields).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each additional field reduces conversion by 20-30%; friction here is the second biggest cause of low signups.
